_publications._publicationTitle: Longitudinal phenotype development in a minipig model of neurofibromatosis type 1.

_contextSnippet: We found statistically smaller long bones (femur, tibia, humerus, ulna, metacarpals) in the NF1+/ex42del minipigs compared to their wildtype siblings.
details: Statistically smaller long bones (femur, tibia, humerus, ulna, metacarpals) in NF1+/ex42del minipigs compared to wildtype siblings, indicating short stature phenotype
